page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, the instance allocates 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU configuration includes a 32 MB L3 cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, with dedicated CPU allocation. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks demonstrate a notable contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance achieves strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks and average Geekbench single-core scores. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is average, with read speeds at 46.82 GB/sec and write speeds at 27.55 GB/sec, though small-block cached read bandwidth falls into the lower tier.

Given its high memory density but constrained multi-core processing power, the c2d-highmem-4 is suited for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute. It performs adequately in single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and text processing, but shows limitations in highly parallelized operations, database transactions, and high-throughput web serving. This makes it a viable option for small-scale in-memory caching, lightweight data processing, and applications requiring a larger memory footprint without the cost of additional vCPUs.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It provides 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core and a dedicated CPU allocation, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals strong single-core CPU performance, alongside average large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core execution and database operations show weaker performance profiles. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand heavy parallel processing, such as small-scale in-memory caching, text processing, and lightweight data analysis.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute needs.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine offering high memory-to-core density for single-threaded and memory-bound workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 4 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per-core memory ratio
Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with dedicated CPU allocation
Strong single-core CPU performance but weaker multi-core scaling
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for small-scale in-memory caching and text processing
